User.title: User
User.description: Represents a user of the TripMind application, storing their profile information and preferences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity.

email.description: Unique email address of the user.

name.description: Display name of the user. Can be null.

avatar.description: URL to the user's avatar image. Can be null.

language.description: Preferred language of the user, e.g., 'fr' for French.

currency.description: Preferred currency of the user, e.g., 'EUR' for Euro.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Session.title: Session
Session.description: Represents an active user session, used for authentication and maintaining user login state.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Session entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User associated with this session. (Relationship: User 1:N Session)

token.description: Unique token used to identify the session.

expiresAt.description: Timestamp when the session will expire.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the session was created.

Trip.title: Trip
Trip.description: Represents a travel plan created by a user, including destinations, dates, and preferences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Trip entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this trip. (Relationship: User 1:N Trip)

title.description: Title or name of the travel plan.

destinations.description: An array of string representing the names of the destinations included in the trip, e.g., ['Paris', 'Lyon'].

startDate.description: The planned start date of the trip.

endDate.description: The planned end date of the trip.

travelers.description: JSON string representing traveler details: { adults: number, children: string[] (ages, e.g., ['5', '8']), hasPets: boolean }.

preferences.description: JSON string representing user preferences for the trip: { pace: number (0-100, relaxed to intense), budget: number (0-100, economic to luxury), interests: string[], accessibility: string[], dietary: string[], alreadyVisited: string[], mustSee: string[] }.

status.description: Current status of the trip (e.g., draft, active, completed, archived).

shareToken.description: Unique token for sharing the trip publicly. Can be null.

deletedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the trip was soft-deleted. Can be null.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the trip was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the trip was last updated.

Day.title: Day
Day.description: Represents a single day within a travel plan, containing a sequence of events.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Day entity.

tripId.description: Reference to the Trip this day belongs to. (Relationship: Trip 1:N Day)

date.description: The specific date of this day in the trip.

orderIndex.description: The sequential order of this day within the trip.

notes.description: Any additional notes or remarks for this day. Can be null.

startLocationName.description: Name of the starting location for the day. Can be null.

startLocationAddress.description: Address of the starting location for the day. Can be null.

startLat.description: Latitude of the starting location for the day. Can be null.

startLng.description: Longitude of the starting location for the day. Can be null.

endLocationName.description: Name of the ending location for the day. Can be null.

endLocationAddress.description: Address of the ending location for the day. Can be null.

endLat.description: Latitude of the ending location for the day. Can be null.

endLng.description: Longitude of the ending location for the day. Can be null.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the day was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the day was last updated.

Event.title: Event
Event.description: Represents a specific activity, visit, or booking within a day of a trip.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Event entity.

dayId.description: Reference to the Day this event belongs to. (Relationship: Day 1:N Event)

type.description: Type of event (e.g., visit, meal, transport, accommodation, activity).

description.description: Detailed description of the event. Can be null.

startTime.description: Planned start time of the event in HH:mm format. Can be null.

durationMinutes.description: Estimated duration of the event in minutes. Can be null.

locationName.description: Name of the location for the event. Can be null.

locationAddress.description: Address of the location for the event. Can be null.

lat.description: Latitude of the event location. Can be null.

lng.description: Longitude of the event location. Can be null.

photos.description: An array of URLs for photos related to the event.

practicalInfo.description: JSON string representing practical information for the event: { openingHours: string, priceRange: string, tips: string[], bestTimeToVisit: string }.

estimatedBudget.description: Estimated budget or cost for the event. Can be null.

isAiEnriched.description: Indicates if the event details have been enriched by AI.

sourceUrl.description: URL to an external source for more information about the event. Can be null.

attachments.description: An array of objects, where each object represents an attached file with properties like id (string), filename (string), originalName (string), mimeType (string), size (number), category ('billet', 'voucher', 'reservation', 'itineraire', 'contrat', 'autre'), and uploadedAt (string, ISO date-time format).

orderIndex.description: The sequential order of this event within its day.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the event was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the event was last updated.

Enrichment.title: Enrichment
Enrichment.description: Stores the raw AI response for event enrichments, allowing for debugging and analysis.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Enrichment entity.

eventId.description: Reference to the Event that was enriched. (Relationship: Event 1:N Enrichment)

rawResponse.description: The complete raw JSON response received from the AI service for the enrichment.

tokensUsed.description: Number of AI tokens used for this enrichment request. Can be null.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the enrichment record was created.

definition.description: Stores user profile information, directly identified by the user's Firebase Authentication UID. Accessible only by the authenticated user matching `userId`.

Stores individual travel plans. These documents are owned by the user identified by `userId` in the path, which matches the `userId` field within the document for direct ownership verification. Includes a `shareToken` field for backend-managed public access.

Represents a single day within a travel plan. Each document includes denormalized `userId` (owner of the trip) and `tripId` for authorization independence, allowing direct ownership checks without querying parent documents.

Represents a specific activity, visit, or booking within a day. Each document includes denormalized `userId` (owner of the trip), `tripId`, and `dayId` for authorization independence, enabling robust and efficient security rules.

Stores raw AI responses for event enrichments. Each document includes denormalized `userId` (owner of the trip), `tripId`, `dayId`, and `eventId` for authorization independence, ensuring self-contained access control.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes Authorization Independence and Query-Aware Security Rules (QAPs) by denormalizing authorization context directly into subcollection documents. User-specific data is organized hierarchically under a `/users/{userId}` root, ensuring that all data for a given user is co-located and secured with simple, path-based rules. 

**Authorization Independence:** To eliminate the need for `get()` calls in security rules (which are critical for atomic operations and debugging), `userId` (the trip owner's ID) is denormalized and included in `Day`, `Event`, and `Enrichment` documents. Additionally, `tripId` is denormalized into `Event` and `Enrichment` documents, and `dayId` into `Enrichment` documents. This ensures that any document in the hierarchy contains all necessary ancestral identifiers to validate ownership and context directly, without querying parent documents. For example, a rule for an `Event` document can simply check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` and `request.path.matches('/users/{userId}/trips/{tripId}/days/{dayId}/events/{eventId}')`.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The hierarchical path structure `/users/{userId}/trips/{tripId}/...` inherently supports QAPs. When a client performs a `list` query on, for example, `/users/{userId}/trips`, the Firestore security rules can enforce that `request.auth.uid == userId` from the path wildcard. This ensures that users can only list *their own* trips, and similarly for days, events, and enrichments. No `list` operation will implicitly return data belonging to other users or data that the current user is not authorized to see, thus avoiding the 'rules are not filters' trap.

**Share Token Handling:** The `shareToken` on the `Trip` entity is designed to be managed by the application's backend API, as indicated by `GET /api/trips/[id]?shareToken=...`. This means the backend validates the `shareToken` and then fetches the trip from Firestore (potentially using service account credentials or an elevated user context). Consequently, Firestore security rules for the `Trip` documents do not need to implement complex logic for public sharing, further simplifying them to primarily enforce user ownership. If direct client-to-Firestore public access via `shareToken` were required, a dedicated top-level `public_trips` collection would be considered, but it's not necessary given the API mediation.

**DBAC and App Settings:** While the core principles advocate for dedicated collections for global roles (e.g., `_roles_admin/{userId}`) and app settings, these have been omitted from the `firestorePaths` array. This is due to the strict schema requirement that `definition.schema.$ref` must refer to an existing entity in `#/backend/entities/<EntityName>`. No `AdminRole` or `AppSetting` entities were provided in the input, preventing their inclusion under the specified output format constraints.
